name: 3-Point Dumbbell Row
cuesSetup: Set shoulders down and back before pulling
Brace through the trunk and support hand
Start each rep from a dead stop or controlled stretch
cuesExecution: Drive elbow toward your hip pocket
Pause briefly at the top; squeeze back
Control the return; don’t let the shoulder dump forward

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
cuesSetup: Set a stable stance and brace your core
Start each rep in a strong, stacked position
Choose a load you can control
cuesExecution: Move smoothly; control the lowering phase
Keep tension on the target muscles
Stop before form breaks

name: Air Squat
cuesSetup: Feet about shoulder-width, toes slightly out
Brace hard before the first rep
Keep ribs stacked over pelvis
cuesExecution: Sit between the hips; keep chest tall
Knees track in line with toes
Control the descent; drive through mid-foot

name: Arnold Press
cuesSetup: Set a stable stance and brace your core
Start each rep in a strong, stacked position

cuesSetup: Set a stable stance and brace your core
Start each rep in a strong, stacked position
Choose a load you can control
cuesExecution: Move smoothly; control the lowering phase
Keep tension on the target muscles
Stop before form breaks

name: Standing Calf Raise
cuesSetup: Full range: stretch at bottom
Use support for balance
Keep knee position consistent
cuesExecution: Pause at the top; squeeze calves
Slow on the way down
Don’t bounce

name: Stationary Bike
cuesSetup: Set a stable stance and brace your core
Start each rep in a strong, stacked position
Choose a load you can control
cuesExecution: Move smoothly; control the lowering phase
Keep tension on the target muscles

name: Step Up
cuesSetup: Tall torso; brace
Front foot flat; knee tracks over toes
Use a stride that lets you control depth
cuesExecution: Lower under control; back knee toward floor
Drive through front heel/mid-foot
Keep hips square; don’t collapse inward

name: Straight Arm Pulldown
cuesSetup: Set shoulders down and back before pulling
Brace; keep torso stable
Start each rep from a dead stop/tension
cuesExecution: Pull elbows toward your pockets
Pause briefly at the top; squeeze back
Control the return; don’t let shoulders dump forward
Pull bar to upper chest; don’t swing

name: Suitcase Carry
cuesSetup: Set a stable stance and brace your core
Start each rep in a strong, stacked position

cuesExecution: Move smoothly; control the lowering phase

name: Sumo Deadlift
cuesSetup: Feet hip-width; bar/weight close to shins
Hinge back to find hamstring tension
Brace and set lats (pull shoulders down/back)
cuesExecution: Push the floor away; keep the weight close
Hips and shoulders rise together
Stand tall without overextending the low back

name: T-Bar Row

Start each rep from a dead stop/tension
cuesExecution: Pull elbows toward your pockets

name: Triceps Dip
cuesSetup: Shoulder blades down and back
Wrists stacked; grip the handle/bar tight
Feet planted; light arch allowed
cuesExecution: Lower with control; elbows ~45° from torso
Press up and back; keep shoulders packed
Stop 1 rep before form breaks
Go as deep as shoulders tolerate

name: Triceps Pushdown
cuesSetup: Elbows fixed; shoulders down
Brace core; avoid flaring ribs
Pick a weight you can control
cuesExecution: Extend fully without locking painfully
Slow eccentric; keep elbows in
Avoid using momentum

name: Weighted Push Up
cuesSetup: Hands under shoulders; body in a straight line

Keep elbows ~30–45°
Press the floor away; finish with locked-out arms

name: Wrist Curl
cuesSetup: Stand tall; ribs down
Elbows pinned near sides
Grip hard; wrists neutral
cuesExecution: Curl without swinging
